description: Free tool that emails you whenever your keyword is mentioned on Reddit. monitor competitor names product names pain point keywords.
use_case: Lead discovery + competitor monitoring + content ideas
compliance_notes: None. Public data monitoring.
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up at f5bot.com 2. Add competitor brand names 3. Add pain point keywords (e.g. 'need a tool for' 'looking for alternative') 4. Add product category terms 5. Check email daily for leads 6. Respond to threads where relevant
reviewer_notes: Free tool. Zero risk. Immediate intel on who is looking for what you sell.

description: Track subreddit growth rates to identify emerging communities before they saturate. rising subreddits = emerging demand.
compliance_notes: None. Public data.
actionable_steps: 1. Go to freesubstats.com 2. Search subreddits in your niches 3. Track growth rate (subscribers/day) 4. Compare to competitor subreddits 5. Identify fast-growing subs (>100 new members/day) 6. Build content strategy around emerging communities
reviewer_notes: Combine with f5bot for full Reddit intel stack. Growth rate > absolute size for opportunity sizing.

description: Search LinkedIn People tab with company filter to find competitor customers. filter by title to find decision makers who already use competing products.
use_case: Competitor customer poaching + warm lead gen
compliance_notes: Don't scrape at scale (LinkedIn TOS). Manual or careful automation with limits. See PLATFORM_AUTOMATION_LIMITS_2026.md
actionable_steps: 1. Go to LinkedIn People tab 2. Add competitor company to 'Current company' filter 3. Filter by relevant job titles (VP Marketing etc) 4. These are PROVEN buyers of competitor product 5. Send connection request with personalized note 6. Follow up with value-first message 7. Pitch your solution
reviewer_notes: These leads already have budget allocated and understand the problem. Highest conversion rate possible.

description: SEC 10-K annual filings contain Risk Factors section. companies legally MUST disclose risks. these are their actual pain points that they would pay to solve.
use_case: Enterprise lead gen + pain point discovery + sales angle generation
compliance_notes: None. Public filings. Companies are legally required to disclose this.
actionable_steps: 1. Go to SEC EDGAR 2. Search target company 10-K filing 3. Ctrl+F 'Risk Factors' section 4. Read every risk they disclose 5. Map risks to your products/services 6. Cold email referencing their specific disclosed risk 7. Example: 'I noticed in your 10-K you disclosed cybersecurity risk. We solve that.'
reviewer_notes: Borderline illegal how good this is. Nobody does this. Company TELLS you their problems in a legal document then you email them the solution. Works for any B2B service.

method_name: FOIA Requests for Government Vendor Contracts
description: Freedom of Information Act requests reveal which vendors government agencies use and how much they pay. use this to find expiring contracts and undercut incumbents.
use_case: Government sales + contract intelligence + competitor pricing
compliance_notes: Must be US-based or have US entity. FOIA is legal right. Response time 20-60 business days.
actionable_steps: 1. Identify target government agency 2. File FOIA request for vendor contracts in your category 3. Ask for: vendor name contract value contract dates scope of work 4. Wait 20-60 business days for response 5. Map expiring contracts (they rebid these) 6. Prepare bid or cold outreach before contract expires 7. Reference current contract terms in your pitch
reviewer_notes: Slow but extremely high value. Government contracts are public record. You know exactly what they pay their current vendor. Long sales cycle but large deal sizes.

description: Searchable database of ALL federal government spending. find which agencies spend on your category and who their current vendors are. no FOIA wait needed.
use_case: Government lead gen + competitive intelligence + market sizing
compliance_notes: None. Public database maintained by US Treasury.
actionable_steps: 1. Go to usaspending.gov 2. Search by keyword in your industry 3. Filter by agency and date range 4. Export spending data 5. Identify agencies spending $100K+ in your category 6. Find current vendors (competitors to displace) 7. Note contract end dates for rebid timing 8. Cold outreach to contracting officers
reviewer_notes: Faster than FOIA. same intel. Start here then FOIA for specific contract details. Government is the biggest customer in the world.

description: AI-powered parallel dialer. dials 5-10 numbers simultaneously and connects you only when a human picks up. 5-10x more conversations per hour vs manual dialing.
use_case: Cold calling at scale + SDR productivity
compliance_notes: Must comply with TCPA. Do not call list scrubbing required. No robocalling - human must be on the line.
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up at orum.com 2. Upload lead list (scrubbed against DNC) 3. Configure parallel dial settings (5-10 lines) 4. Prep call script 5. Start dialing - system connects you when human answers 6. Log disposition in CRM 7. Follow up with email after conversation
reviewer_notes: Premium but ROI positive if your deal size is >$500. VA + parallel dialer = $300/mo for 10x productivity.

description: AI parallel dialer with virtual floor for SDR teams. includes AI note-taking and CRM integration. designed for team environments.
use_case: Cold calling at scale + team SDR ops
compliance_notes: Same TCPA compliance as LG007.
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up at nooks.ai 2. Import lead lists 3. Set up virtual sales floor 4. Configure parallel lines 5. AI takes notes during calls automatically 6. Dispositions sync to CRM 7. Review AI call summaries for coaching
reviewer_notes: Best for teams. AI note-taking is the differentiator. If solo use Orum or PhoneBurner instead.

description: Enterprise parallel dialer. AI identifies voicemail vs human. connects only live answers. includes local presence dialing (calls from local area codes).
compliance_notes: TCPA compliant. Local presence dialing may have state-specific regulations.
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up at koncert.com 2. Upload scrubbed lead list 3. Enable local presence dialing (calls from their area code) 4. Set parallel lines (5-8) 5. AI detects voicemail vs human 6. Only connects you to live humans 7. Log in CRM 8. Follow up sequence
reviewer_notes: Local presence dialing increases answer rate 2-3x. Enterprise-grade. Good for high-ticket B2B.

description: Power dialer with voicemail drop. not true parallel but very fast sequential dialing. includes pre-recorded voicemail drops and email follow-up automation.
use_case: Solo SDR cold calling + voicemail drops
compliance_notes: TCPA compliant. Voicemail drops are generally legal (you are calling not texting).
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up at phoneburner.com 2. Upload leads 3. Record voicemail drop message 4. Configure email follow-up templates 5. Start power dialing 6. Drop voicemail on no-answer (1 click) 7. Send email follow-up automatically 8. Track in built-in CRM
reviewer_notes: Best bang for buck for solopreneurs. $149/mo. Voicemail drop + email follow-up in one tool. Not parallel but fast enough for solo ops.

description: Ringless voicemail delivery. deposits voicemail directly without ringing the phone. higher listen rate than cold calls (people check voicemails).
use_case: Outreach at scale without live calling
compliance_notes: Ringless voicemail legality varies by state. FCC considers it a call under TCPA. MUST have prior express consent OR use for informational (not marketing) purposes. Scrub against DNC list. Check state laws.
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up at drop.co 2. Record compelling voicemail (15-30 seconds) 3. Upload lead list (DNC scrubbed) 4. Send voicemail drops in batches 5. Track listen rates and callbacks 6. Follow up callbacks immediately 7. A/B test voicemail scripts
reviewer_notes: Legal grey area. Some states restrict ringless voicemail. Check compliance before scaling. Good for warm leads where you have some prior relationship.

description: AI-generated personalized voicemail drops. clone your voice then send personalized voicemails at scale with prospect name and company.
compliance_notes: Same TCPA concerns as LG011. AI voice cloning adds FTC disclosure considerations if voice is synthetic.
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up at voicedrop.ai 2. Record voice sample for cloning 3. Create voicemail template with personalization variables 4. Upload lead list with name + company 5. AI generates personalized voicemail per prospect 6. Send drops in batches 7. Track callbacks 8. Follow up immediately
reviewer_notes: AI personalization at scale. Each prospect gets voicemail with their name. Higher callback rate than generic drops. Same compliance concerns.

description: Leads generated through co-registration on other sites. user opts in on Site A and consents to be contacted by partners. provides TCPA-compliant consent chain.
use_case: Compliant lead gen for cold calling + SMS
compliance_notes: Consent must be clear and documented. FCC 1:1 consent rule (Jan 2025) requires lead to specifically consent to YOUR company. Generic 'partners may contact you' no longer sufficient.
actionable_steps: 1. Find co-registration lead providers in your niche 2. Verify consent language includes YOUR specific company name 3. Verify consent is documented and auditable 4. Test small batch (100 leads) 5. Track conversion rate vs cost per lead 6. Scale winning sources 7. Keep consent records for 5+ years
reviewer_notes: FCC tightened rules Jan 2025. 1:1 consent now required. Old co-reg leads (generic consent) are NOW non-compliant. Only buy leads where YOUR company is specifically named in consent.

description: Run sweepstakes/giveaway where entry requires providing phone/email + consent to be contacted. creates TCPA-compliant consent chain you control.
compliance_notes: Must have official sweepstakes rules. No purchase necessary required. State-specific registration may be needed for prizes >$500. Consent language must be specific.
actionable_steps: 1. Choose prize relevant to your target market 2. Create sweepstakes landing page 3. Entry form: name email phone + TCPA consent checkbox 4. Consent text names YOUR company specifically 5. Run Meta/TikTok ads to landing page 6. Collect leads with documented consent 7. Call/text leads with compliant messaging 8. Follow up with product offer
reviewer_notes: Legal and effective. Prize should attract your ICP not random people. A $100 gift card attracts everyone. A specific industry tool attracts your buyers.

description: Prospect texts a keyword to your shortcode to opt in. creates rock-solid TCPA consent because THEY initiated. works in ads social bios and content CTAs.
use_case: Compliant SMS lead gen + list building
compliance_notes: Strongest TCPA consent possible - prospect initiates. Must still include required disclosures in auto-reply. CTIA compliance required for shortcodes.
actionable_steps: 1. Get SMS shortcode or 10DLC number (Twilio Postscript etc) 2. Set up keyword trigger (e.g. text GROWTH to 55555) 3. Configure auto-reply with disclosure + first message 4. Promote keyword in ads bio content CTAs 5. Build SMS list of fully opted-in leads 6. Send value-first SMS sequence 7. Convert to calls or sales
reviewer_notes: Gold standard for TCPA compliance. They texted YOU first. Put keyword CTA in every piece of content. Pair with reply bait posts.

description: Interactive quiz that qualifies leads and captures contact info with consent. prospect self-selects their problem and provides contact to get results.
use_case: Qualified lead gen + segmentation + TCPA consent
compliance_notes: Include TCPA consent checkbox before results are shown. Quiz results page is the consent exchange.
actionable_steps: 1. Build quiz relevant to your ICP (e.g. 'What's your biggest growth bottleneck?') 2. 5-7 questions that segment the lead 3. Results page requires name + phone + email + TCPA consent 4. Auto-deliver results via email 5. Call leads same day 6. Reference their quiz answers in conversation 7. Track which quiz paths convert best
reviewer_notes: Leads are pre-qualified by their own answers. You know their problem before calling. Higher conversion than cold lists. Quiz platforms: Typeform Interact Outgrow.

description: Monitors competitor web pages for changes. price changes hiring pages product updates feature removals. alerts within minutes of any change.
use_case: Competitive intelligence + outreach timing + market monitoring
compliance_notes: None. Monitoring public web pages is legal.
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up at visualping.io 2. Add competitor pricing pages 3. Add competitor career pages 4. Add competitor product/feature pages 5. Set check frequency (hourly or daily) 6. When alert fires: price increase = their customers may switch. New hires = expansion signal. Feature removal = gap in market 7. Outreach within 24 hours of change
reviewer_notes: @pipelineabuser monitors 200+ pages. borderline illegal how much intel this gives you. price increase alerts = instant outreach angle.

description: Target 1-2 star reviewers of competitor products. these people have budget (already paying for category) AND documented pain points.
compliance_notes: None. Public reviews on public platforms.
actionable_steps: 1. Search G2/Capterra/Trustpilot for competitor products 2. Filter by 1-2 star reviews 3. Note specific complaints 4. Find reviewer on LinkedIn (often same name) 5. Outreach: 'Saw your review mentioning [pain point]. We specialize in fixing that.' 6. Time outreach within 2 weeks of review
reviewer_notes: People who leave bad reviews are actively unhappy AND have budget. Highest intent leads possible.

description: Search for companies sued for issues you prevent. cybersecurity breach lawsuits = security product leads. employment lawsuits = HR tool leads.
compliance_notes: None. Public court records. PACER is official US courts database. CourtListener is free alternative.
actionable_steps: 1. Search PACER or CourtListener for lawsuits in your category 2. Identify companies sued for issues your product prevents 3. Research the company on LinkedIn 4. Outreach: 'Noticed recent litigation around [issue]. Our clients avoid this because...' 5. Very targeted and high-intent
reviewer_notes: Niche but powerful. Companies that got sued for something you prevent = urgency + budget + pain. Nobody else is doing this.

description: Alerts on funding rounds. companies that just raised = money to spend + pressure to deploy it. outreach within 1-4 weeks post-announcement.
compliance_notes: None. Public funding data.
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up Crunchbase (free tier works) 2. Set alerts for Series A/B in target verticals 3. Outreach within 1-2 weeks of announcement 4. Angle: Congrats on the round. Companies at this stage usually invest in [your category] 5. Track conversion by funding stage
reviewer_notes: 1-4 weeks post-funding is the sweet spot. They have money AND pressure. Best timing signal for B2B outbound.

description: Companies hiring for roles your product replaces or augments = pre-qualified leads. hiring content writer = sell AI writing tool. hiring SDRs = sell outbound automation.
compliance_notes: None. Public job postings.
actionable_steps: 1. Search job boards for roles your product replaces 2. Hiring SDRs = outbound infra need 3. Hiring content writers = AI content opportunity 4. Hiring data analysts = analytics tool sale 5. Outreach while job is still posted 6. Angle: Saw you're hiring for [role]. Before you scale that team...
reviewer_notes: Companies hiring = budget allocated + active pain point. The job posting IS their pain point documentation.

description: New hires are 6x more likely to buy. filter by job change in last 90 days. they want quick wins and are open to new approaches.
compliance_notes: None. Using Sales Navigator as designed.
actionable_steps: 1. Open Sales Navigator 2. Filter: job changes last 90 days 3. Add company growth and tech stack filters 4. New decision makers want quick wins 5. Outreach during 30-90 day onboarding window 6. Angle: Congrats on the new role. When leaders step into [role] they usually find [pain point]...
reviewer_notes: 6x more likely to buy. The science: new executives have 90-day mandates to show impact. They need quick wins. Your product is the quick win.

description: Search for people actively asking for alternatives or expressing pain with competitors. real-time intent signals from public posts.
compliance_notes: None. Public tweets.
actionable_steps: 1. Use X advanced search 2. Query: 'recommend OR alternative OR switch from [competitor]' 3. Add filter:has_engagement min_faves:5 4. Reply to hand-raisers with genuine help 5. DM if appropriate 6. Track which search queries yield best leads
reviewer_notes: People literally tweeting 'looking for an alternative to X' and you can reply in real time. Free. Immediate.

description: Free alert for any keyword mentioned on the web. competitor brand mentions industry keywords pain point phrases.
use_case: Competitive monitoring + content opportunities + lead signals
compliance_notes: None. Public web monitoring.
actionable_steps: 1. Go to google.com/alerts 2. Add: competitor brand names 3. Add: industry keywords 4. Add: pain point phrases 5. Set frequency: daily digest 6. Reply to relevant mentions 7. Identify content opportunities
reviewer_notes: Free and takes 10 minutes. Not as real-time as f5bot for Reddit but covers the entire web.

description: Monitor Show HN for competitor launches. Track Ask HN for pain points. high-quality technical audience with buying power.
compliance_notes: None. Public forum.
actionable_steps: 1. Monitor Show HN daily for launches in your space 2. Track Ask HN for pain point discussions 3. Engage genuinely with technical depth 4. Set up f5bot alerts for HN keywords 5. Respond within hours for maximum visibility
reviewer_notes: Technical audience with buying power. First place many developer tools get discovered. Good for MCP server products.

description: Send personalized voice notes via social DMs. 40%+ reply rate on LinkedIn. 3x engagement vs text DMs. pattern interrupt + trust building.
compliance_notes: None. Using platform features as designed.
actionable_steps: 1. Identify prospect on LinkedIn/IG 2. Research their recent posts or company news 3. Record 45-60 second voice note 4. Mention something specific about them 5. Ask one clear question 6. Send 7. Follow up with text if no reply in 48h
reviewer_notes: @pipelineabuser says best outbound campaigns in 2026 are cold DM > voice note > email followup. Voice note is doing stupid numbers right now.

description: Personalized Loom videos for high-value prospects. show their website on screen. 40%+ reply rates. only worth it for $10K+ deal sizes.
compliance_notes: None. Standard sales practice.
actionable_steps: 1. Research prospect deeply 2. Open their website on screen 3. Record 45-60 second Loom 4. Walk through specific observation about their site/business 5. Suggest one improvement 6. CTA: 15-minute call 7. Send via email with thumbnail showing their name
reviewer_notes: 40%+ reply rates documented. Thumbnail showing prospect name increases open rate. Only use for deals worth $10K+ to justify the time investment.

method_name: Cold DM > Voice Note > Email Sequence
description: Multi-channel sequence: start with social DM then voice note then email. 2.5x reply rate vs email alone. builds familiarity across touchpoints.
compliance_notes: Each channel used within platform rules. CAN-SPAM compliant emails.
actionable_steps: 1. Day 1: Cold DM on LinkedIn with specific observation 2. Day 3: Follow up with personalized voice note 3. Day 5: Email referencing both previous touchpoints 4. Day 8: Final follow-up email with case study 5. Track which channel gets the response
reviewer_notes: @pipelineabuser confirmed: best outbound campaigns are cold DM > voice note > email. Not just email anymore. Multi-channel builds familiarity.

description: AI enrichment platform. connect 50+ data providers. auto-enrich leads with intent signals company data tech stack. AI writes personalization.
use_case: Lead enrichment + AI personalization + signal-based timing
compliance_notes: None. Aggregates public data from multiple sources.
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up Clay 2. Connect data sources (Apollo LinkedIn GitHub etc) 3. Define enrichment waterfall 4. Auto-enrich: company data > tech stack > intent signals > recent news 5. AI generates personalized first lines 6. Route enriched leads to Instantly for sending 7. Track which enrichment signals convert best
reviewer_notes: The backbone of modern cold email in 2026. AI writes 80% of personalization. Signal-based timing not spray and pray. @pipelineabuser recommended stack.

description: Shows what technology websites use. find leads based on their tools. selling Shopify apps? Find Shopify stores. competitor tool users = displacement targets.
compliance_notes: None. Public technology data.
actionable_steps: 1. Use BuiltWith or free Wappalyzer Chrome extension 2. Search for companies using competitor tools 3. Search for companies using complementary tech 4. Pre-qualified: they already use the category 5. Outreach: 'I see you use X. Companies using X often struggle with Y. We fix that.'
reviewer_notes: Budget option: Wappalyzer free Chrome extension for manual research. BuiltWith for bulk. Competitor tool users are the warmest cold leads.

description: Search Facebook Ads Library for active ads in your category. if someone is paying for ads = they are making money. reverse-engineer winning funnels.
use_case: Product validation + funnel intelligence + competitor research
compliance_notes: None. Public ads database provided by Meta.
actionable_steps: 1. Go to FB Ads Library 2. Search: Ebook Digital Download PDF Printable Template Guide Course Planner 3. Filter by active ads running 2+ months 4. Analyze: ad creative > landing page > product > pricing 5. Reverse-engineer the winning funnel 6. Clone approach for your niche
reviewer_notes: @xivy0k method. If someone is paying for ads for 2+ months they are making money. The Ads Library is the best free market research tool.

description: Shows trending apps and new big hits in app stores. identify viral apps to clone/adapt for niche markets.
use_case: App discovery + clone opportunities + market timing
compliance_notes: None. Public app store data.
actionable_steps: 1. Check appkittie.com daily 2. Go to New Big Hits section 3. Identify apps gaining traction 4. Cross-reference with niche opportunities 5. Build adapted version (faith/fitness/student) 6. Ship within 2-4 weeks of trend identification
reviewer_notes: Christian app example: 1700 reviews and $100K+ revenue in 5 days. Daily monitoring is essential for APP_FACTORY method.

description: @gregisenberg tool. AI agents scan Reddit and FB Groups for validated problems. frustrated customers with problems waiting for solutions.
use_case: Idea validation + demand discovery + problem identification
compliance_notes: None. Scans public community data.
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up for IdeaBrowser 2. Set filters for target niches (faith fitness AI productivity) 3. Review daily signals 4. AI identifies frustrated customers with specific pain points 5. Test top opportunities 6. Build solutions for validated problems
reviewer_notes: @gregisenberg recommended. Find validated problems before building solutions. Reduces risk of building something nobody wants.

description: Monitor daily launches for market signals and competitive intelligence. study what gets upvoted positioning messaging pricing.
use_case: Market intelligence + competitive analysis + launch timing
compliance_notes: None. Public platform.
actionable_steps: 1. Monitor PH daily for launches in your space 2. Study: what gets upvoted positioning messaging pricing 3. Note which categories get most engagement 4. Track failed launches (market signal) 5. Use for timing your own launches 6. Tuesday-Thursday 12:01 AM PT = optimal launch time
reviewer_notes: Product of the Day = 2000-5000 signups average. Study what works before your own launch.

description: Heavy research into TikTok trends and App Store movers. build apps around validated trends with existing audiences.
compliance_notes: None. Public platform research.
actionable_steps: 1. Monitor TikTok trends daily 2. Search relevant hashtags in your niche 3. Cross-reference with appkittie App Store movers 4. Identify viral concepts that could become apps 5. Partner with niche TikTok influencers for distribution 6. Build for existing audiences not hypothetical ones
reviewer_notes: Connor Burd $185K/mo from app portfolio using this exact research method. Heavy TikTok research + App Store cross-reference.

description: Reddit provides 68% of AI answer citations. GEO-optimized posts get cited by ChatGPT/Claude/Perplexity driving organic traffic.
use_case: SEO + AI citation optimization + organic traffic
compliance_notes: Must follow subreddit rules. Value-first not spam.
actionable_steps: 1. Identify relevant subreddits 2. Create value-first posts with helpful content 3. Include natural links to your site/product 4. Use specific data and structured formatting (AI loves tables and lists) 5. Answer questions thoroughly 6. Your posts get cited in AI responses
reviewer_notes: Reddit is source #1 for AI answer citations (68%). Your Reddit posts become AI training data and get cited in ChatGPT/Claude responses.

description: Pay 300-500 clippers $1/1K views to test hooks simultaneously. volume testing beats single influencer gambles.
use_case: Content distribution at scale + hook testing
compliance_notes: Standard influencer/affiliate payment model. No legal issues.
actionable_steps: 1. Build clipper roster via DMs/Whop 2. Distribute content briefs 3. Pay performance-based ($1/1K views) 4. Double down on winning hooks 5. Scale across network 6. Case study: 43K downloads for $6K
reviewer_notes: @tatealax documented: 43K downloads for $6K spend. 300-500 accounts testing hooks simultaneously. Volume testing beats betting on one influencer.

description: Build 1000+ landing pages targeting long-tail keywords. each page = app feature + niche/location variation.
use_case: Organic traffic at scale + long-tail keyword capture
compliance_notes: Standard SEO. No black hat.
actionable_steps: 1. List all product features 2. Cross with niches locations professions 3. Create page template 4. Generate 1000+ variations programmatically 5. Internal link to main product page 6. Each targets a unique long-tail keyword
reviewer_notes: @Hightrafficsite method. Example: 'habit tracker for nurses' + 'habit tracker for teachers' + 1000 more variations. Each page captures unique search traffic.

description: Self-hosted unlimited workflows at $20/mo server cost. replaces Zapier at 10x scale. best for content repurposing and lead enrichment.
compliance_notes: None. Self-hosted on your own server.
actionable_steps: 1. Deploy n8n on $20/mo Hetzner VPS 2. Build content repurposing workflow (1 piece > 6 platforms) 3. Build lead enrichment pipeline 4. Build email sequence triggers 5. Scale without per-task costs 6. Replaces Zapier/Make at flat cost
reviewer_notes: Saves $51/mo vs Zapier/Make/Buffer. Unlimited workflows. Docker setup guide in AUTOMATIONS/N8N_SETUP_AND_WORKFLOWS.md

description: 15-30 days pure value emails then one flash sale = $15-45K in 48 hours. works with just 200 subscribers.
compliance_notes: CAN-SPAM compliant with opt-in list and unsubscribe link.
actionable_steps: 1. Build email list with daily value content 2. No pitching for 15-30 days 3. Drop one flash sale email 4. $15-45K in 48 hours documented 5. Even 200 subscribers can generate $10K+ drops 6. Return to value mode after
reviewer_notes: @knoxtwts documented: works with 200 subs. The list IS the product. The course is just the monetization event.

description: Give month free for referral. viral loop built into app. every user becomes a distribution channel.
compliance_notes: Standard app feature.
actionable_steps: 1. Build referral system: user shares link > friend downloads > both rewarded 2. Track with Branch deep links 3. Reward: free month for both referrer and friend 4. Surface referral option at moments of delight 5. Track viral coefficient (referrals per user)
reviewer_notes: Lowest CAC of any growth channel. Powers exponential growth if viral coefficient >1.

description: Target conference attendees pre/during/post event. monitor hashtag. DM people posting about relevant sessions.
compliance_notes: Standard networking. No deception.
actionable_steps: 1. Identify upcoming conferences in your industry 2. Monitor event hashtags before during after 3. DM people posting about sessions relevant to your product 4. Reference their specific post/session 5. Follow up within 3 days 6. People at events are in active learning/buying mode
reviewer_notes: Conference attendees are in active buying mode. Monitoring hashtags lets you find warm leads without attending.

description: Podcast guests are thought leaders with influence and budget. reference their specific episode for warm outreach.
compliance_notes: None. Public podcast data.
actionable_steps: 1. Search Listen Notes for podcasts in your niche 2. Identify guests (not hosts) who match your ICP 3. Listen to their episode 4. Reference specific insight from the episode in outreach 5. Much higher response rate than generic cold email
reviewer_notes: Podcast guests appreciate the personal touch. They know you actually listened. High-trust outreach method.

description: Search Amazon reviews for office products mentioning 'team' or 'office upgrade'. cross-ref reviewers to LinkedIn. signal: company is scaling.
compliance_notes: None. Public Amazon reviews.
actionable_steps: 1. Search Amazon for office/productivity products 2. Read reviews mentioning 'team' 'scaling' 'office upgrade' 3. Cross-reference reviewer names to LinkedIn 4. Identify the scaling company 5. Outreach: 'Scaling fast? We help companies at your stage with...'
reviewer_notes: Creative and unique. @pipelineabuser gem. Nobody else is mining Amazon reviews for B2B leads. Unproven at scale but zero cost to test.

description: View target profiles 4-7 times over 2 weeks. builds familiarity. they view/connect first. warms relationship before outreach.
compliance_notes: None. Using LinkedIn as designed. Profile viewing is normal behavior.
actionable_steps: 1. Create list of 50 target prospects 2. View 5-10 profiles per day 3. Rotate through list over 2 weeks 4. Many will view your profile back 5. Some will send connection request first 6. When you do outreach it feels warm not cold
reviewer_notes: Subtle but effective. @pipelineabuser method. Creates familiarity before the first message. They recognize your name.

description: Best free tier in B2B databases. 50 email credits/month. 275M+ contacts. built-in sequencing. start here before paying for anything.
compliance_notes: CAN-SPAM compliant with proper opt-out.
actionable_steps: 1. Sign up Apollo.io free tier 2. Use advanced filters to narrow ICP 3. 50 email credits/month free 4. Verify emails before sending 5. Export to verify with NeverBounce 6. Use built-in sequencing for follow-ups 7. Upgrade only when free credits run out
reviewer_notes: Start here. Free. 275M+ contacts. Good enough for initial outreach before investing in Clay or paid tools.

description: Join Slack/Discord/Reddit communities. answer questions publicly. private DM for warm pitch. value-first not spam.
compliance_notes: Must follow community rules. No direct spam. Some communities ban promotional content. Value-first or get banned.
actionable_steps: 1. Join target niche Slack/Discord/Reddit communities 2. Spend 2 weeks providing genuine value 3. Answer questions publicly with real expertise 4. When someone has a problem you solve: DM with 'Had same issue. Switched to [solution]. DM if want details' 5. Never spam. Never pitch publicly.
reviewer_notes: Works when done genuinely. Gets you banned when done as spam. @pipelineabuser: provide real value publicly then DM privately.

description: Tracks terminated YouTube channels. terminated channel = content gap in market. audience looking for replacement.
use_case: Content gap discovery + channel opportunity identification
compliance_notes: None. Public YouTube data.
actionable_steps: 1. Check algrow.online daily 2. Filter for terminated channels in your niche 3. Analyze what content they produced 4. Create similar content to capture abandoned audience 5. Fast-mover advantage for the content gap
reviewer_notes: Niche but zero-cost. Terminated channel = audience looking for a new source. Content gap arbitrage.

description: Discover micro-influencers 10K-100K followers in any niche. filter by engagement rate platform content type. 60% higher engagement than macro.
compliance_notes: None. Public influencer data.
actionable_steps: 1. Use FindMeCreators to search niche 2. Filter by engagement >3% 3. Build roster of 10-50 nano creators 4. Product seeding ($0) or paid ($50-200/video) 5. Track performance per creator 6. Scale winners. Cut losers.
reviewer_notes: @iamgdsa tool. Micro-influencers have 60% higher engagement than macro. Better ROI at bootstrap budget.

description: Hire people with ID-verified LinkedIn accounts as BDRs. connect to HeyReach for automation. 800 messages/week per account.
use_case: LinkedIn outbound at scale + rate limit bypass
compliance_notes: LinkedIn TOS risk if misrepresented. Must use real people with real accounts. Legal as employment but may violate LinkedIn TOS if accounts appear automated.
actionable_steps: 1. Recruit 5-10 people with established LinkedIn profiles 2. Pay $100/month per person 3. Connect accounts to HeyReach 4. 800 messages/week per account 5. Provide scripts and targeting criteria 6. Monitor for LinkedIn restrictions 7. A/B test messaging across accounts
reviewer_notes: @codyschneiderxx experimental approach. Creative way to scale LinkedIn outreach beyond single-account limits. Monitor for TOS enforcement.
